description: Add configSchema with a url-type instanceUrl setting to the Grafana plugin so users with self-hosted, AWS-hosted, or custom-domain Grafana instances can configure their URL. Publish the updated plugin.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/grafana && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add configSchema with instanceUrl to Grafana plugin
userStories.description: Add a configSchema with a required url-type instanceUrl field to the Grafana plugin class and package.json, and set urlPatterns to an empty array so the platform derives match patterns from the configured URL at runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: GrafanaPlugin class declares configSchema with instanceUrl of type 'url' and required: true
package.json opentabs.configSchema matches the class declaration
package.json opentabs.urlPatterns is an empty array []
GrafanaPlugin class urlPatterns is an empty array []
package.json opentabs.homepage is removed (derived from the configured URL at runtime)

userStories.title: Bump Grafana plugin version and publish
userStories.description: Bump the Grafana plugin version in package.json from 0.0.78 to 0.0.79 and run npm publish to publish the updated package.
acceptanceCriteria: npm publish succeeds (the qualityChecks build step validates the package builds correctly)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/grafana/package.json (bump version from 0.0.78 to 0.0.79)

Context:
After the configSchema changes land in US-001, the plugin needs a version bump and publish so users can get the update.

Change in package.json:
```json
"version": "0.0.78"
```
to:
```json
"version": "0.0.79"
```

After building successfully, run:
```bash
npm publish
```

Constraints:
- Only bump the version in plugins/grafana/package.json — do NOT bump any platform packages or other plugins.
- Do NOT change the @opentabs-dev/* dependency versions — they should stay at ^0.0.78.
